rules.regex: E: Unable to locate package (.+)

solutions.title: Install the missing package
solutions.explanation: The Docker build failed because a required package is not installed. Add the package to your Dockerfile.

solutions.title: Use a different base image
solutions.explanation: Consider using a base image that includes the required package, like python:3.11 instead of python:3.11-slim.

solutions.title: Check package name
solutions.explanation: The package name might be different in the container's Linux distribution. Search for the correct package name.

rules.regex: COPY failed: file not found in build context or excluded by .dockerignore: stat (.+): file does not exist

solutions.title: Check file path in COPY instruction
solutions.explanation: The file or directory specified in COPY does not exist. Verify the path is correct relative to the build context.

solutions.title: Check .dockerignore
solutions.explanation: The file might be excluded by .dockerignore. Review your .dockerignore file and remove the exclusion if needed.

solutions.title: Verify build context
solutions.explanation: Ensure you're running docker build from the correct directory with the right build context.

rules.regex: unknown instruction: (.+)

solutions.title: Fix Dockerfile instruction typo
solutions.explanation: Check for typos in Dockerfile keywords like FROM, RUN, COPY, etc.

patterns.name: Docker Invalid or Inaccessible Base Image

rules.regex: manifest unknown|pull access denied|denied: requested access to the resource is denied|Error response from daemon: pull access denied

solutions.title: Verify image name and tag
solutions.explanation: The base image name or tag may be incorrect. Check the image exists on Docker Hub or your registry.

solutions.title: Use a public image
solutions.explanation: If using a private image, ensure you're logged in with docker login and have access.

solutions.title: Pin a specific tag
solutions.explanation: Avoid using :latest. Pin to a specific version like python:3.11.2-slim.

rules.regex: port is already allocated|bind: address already in use|listen tcp.*: address already in use

solutions.title: Change the exposed port
solutions.explanation: The port is already in use. Change the port mapping in your docker run or docker-compose.yml.

solutions.title: Stop conflicting container
solutions.explanation: Stop the container using the port: docker ps to find it, then docker stop <container_id>.

solutions.title: Use dynamic port mapping
solutions.explanation: Map to a random host port: docker run -p 3000 (omit host port).

rules.regex: Permission denied|EACCES|Operation not permitted

solutions.title: Fix file ownership in Dockerfile
solutions.explanation: Add USER directive or chown files: RUN chown -R app:app /app

solutions.title: Run as non-root user
solutions.explanation: Create and use a non-root user in your Dockerfile for security.

solutions.title: Check volume mount permissions
solutions.explanation: If using volumes, ensure the host directory has correct permissions.

rules.regex: failed to export layer|failed to compute cache key|error building layer

solutions.title: Clear Docker build cache
solutions.explanation: Corrupted cache. Clear it and rebuild.

solutions.title: Use --no-cache for clean build
solutions.explanation: Build without cache to avoid corrupted layer state.

solutions.title: Reorder Dockerfile instructions
solutions.explanation: Put rarely-changing instructions first to improve cache hits.

rules.regex: failed to solve with frontend|failed to solve: process \"(.+)\" did not complete successfully

solutions.title: Verify stage names
solutions.explanation: Ensure COPY --from= references an existing stage name.

solutions.title: Check intermediate stage builds
solutions.explanation: Each stage must build successfully. Check the failing stage's instructions.

solutions.title: Simplify build
solutions.explanation: Temporarily reduce to single-stage build to isolate the issue.

rules.regex: network unreachable|Could not resolve host|Temporary failure in name resolution|ETIMEDOUT|ENOTFOUND

solutions.title: Check DNS in CI
solutions.explanation: CI runners may have restricted network. Configure DNS or use --network=host for build.

solutions.title: Retry the build
solutions.explanation: Transient network issue. Retry the pipeline.

solutions.title: Use buildkit
solutions.explanation: DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 can improve network handling during build.

patterns.name: Docker No Space Left on Device

rules.regex: no space left on device|ENOSPC|disk full

solutions.title: Clean Docker system
solutions.explanation: Remove unused images, containers, and build cache to free space.

solutions.title: Increase disk space
solutions.explanation: CI runners may have limited disk. Request more or use larger runners.

solutions.title: Use smaller base images
solutions.explanation: Switch to alpine or slim variants to reduce image size.

rules.regex: exec format error|exec user process caused|invalid architecture

solutions.title: Match platform
solutions.explanation: Image was built for different architecture (e.g. arm64 vs amd64). Use --platform=linux/amd64 if needed.

solutions.title: Use multi-arch base image
solutions.explanation: Ensure your base image supports your target platform.

solutions.title: Build for target platform
solutions.explanation: Explicitly build for the target platform.

rules.regex: error getting credentials|docker-credential.* returned error|could not find credentials

solutions.title: Login to registry
solutions.explanation: Authenticate with the container registry before pulling.

solutions.title: Configure credentials in CI
solutions.explanation: Store registry credentials as GitHub Actions secrets and use them in the workflow.

solutions.title: Check config.json
solutions.explanation: Remove or fix credsStore in ~/.docker/config.json if misconfigured.

rules.regex: excluded by .dockerignore|when matching.*dockerignore

solutions.title: Update .dockerignore
solutions.explanation: Remove the pattern that excludes the file you need in the build context.

solutions.title: Use exception rule
solutions.explanation: Add !filename to include a file that would be excluded by a pattern.

solutions.title: Verify build context
solutions.explanation: Ensure the file exists in the directory used as build context.

rules.regex: Container killed|Killed|Out of memory|OOMKilled|exit code 137

solutions.title: Increase memory limit
solutions.explanation: Add memory limit in docker-compose or k8s: memory: 2g

solutions.title: Optimize application memory
solutions.explanation: Reduce memory usage in the application (e.g. NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=2048).

solutions.title: Check for memory leaks
solutions.explanation: Profile the application for memory leaks.

rules.regex: Cannot locate Dockerfile|dockerfile not found|unable to prepare context

solutions.title: Specify Dockerfile path
solutions.explanation: Use -f to point to the correct Dockerfile location.

solutions.title: Run from correct directory
solutions.explanation: Ensure you're in the directory containing the Dockerfile.

solutions.title: Check GitHub Actions path
solutions.explanation: In CI, use working-directory or correct paths for the Dockerfile.

rules.regex: npm ERR! 404 Not Found - GET https://registry.npmjs.org/(.+) - Not found

solutions.title: Check package name spelling
solutions.explanation: The package name might be misspelled. Search for the correct package name.

solutions.title: Package might be deprecated or removed
solutions.explanation: The package may have been unpublished from npm. Look for an alternative package.

solutions.title: Check if it's a private package
solutions.explanation: If this is a private package, ensure you're authenticated and have access.

rules.regex: npm ERR! peer dep missing: (.+), required by (.+)

solutions.title: Install with legacy peer deps
solutions.explanation: Force npm to ignore peer dependency conflicts. Use this as a quick fix.

solutions.title: Install the missing peer dependency
solutions.explanation: Install the required peer dependency manually at the correct version.

solutions.title: Update conflicting packages
solutions.explanation: Update packages to compatible versions that satisfy peer dependencies.

patterns.name: NPM No Space Left on Device

rules.regex: npm ERR! ENOSPC: no space left on device

solutions.title: Clean npm cache
solutions.explanation: Free up disk space by cleaning npm cache and old build artifacts.

rules.regex: ETIMEDOUT|ECONNRESET|network timeout|request to .* failed

solutions.title: Increase timeout
solutions.explanation: Increase the fetch timeout for slow networks.

solutions.title: Use registry mirror
solutions.explanation: Try a mirror if the main registry is unreachable.

solutions.title: Retry the install
solutions.explanation: Transient network issue. Retry the pipeline or npm install.

rules.regex: ERESOLVE unable to resolve dependency tree|Could not resolve dependency

solutions.title: Use legacy peer deps
solutions.explanation: Bypass peer dependency resolution conflicts.

solutions.title: Use overrides
solutions.explanation: Add overrides in package.json to force specific versions.

solutions.title: Delete lockfile and reinstall
solutions.explanation: Fresh resolution may resolve conflicts (backup first).

rules.regex: gyp ERR!|node-gyp|Error: Can't find Python|MSBUILD : error

solutions.title: Install build tools
solutions.explanation: Install python, make, g++ on Linux. On Windows: npm install --global windows-build-tools

solutions.title: Use prebuilt binaries
solutions.explanation: Try npm rebuild or ensure node version matches prebuilt binaries.

solutions.title: Use Docker for CI
solutions.explanation: Use a CI image with build tools pre-installed (e.g. node:20).

rules.regex: conflict in package-lock|merge conflict in package-lock|lockfileVersion

solutions.title: Regenerate lockfile
solutions.explanation: Delete and regenerate the lockfile to resolve conflicts.

solutions.title: Use npm ci after resolving
solutions.explanation: After fixing, use npm ci for deterministic installs in CI.

solutions.title: Resolve git merge
solutions.explanation: If merge conflict, resolve manually and regenerate lockfile.

rules.regex: JavaScript heap out of memory|FATAL ERROR: Reached heap limit

solutions.title: Increase Node memory
solutions.explanation: Increase the Node.js heap size for npm install.

solutions.title: Use npm ci
solutions.explanation: npm ci is faster and uses less memory than npm install.

solutions.title: Install in stages
solutions.explanation: Install dependencies in smaller batches if possible.

rules.regex: Unsupported engine|error EBADENGINE|not matching your node

solutions.title: Upgrade Node version
solutions.explanation: Use a Node version that satisfies the package's engine requirement.

solutions.title: Use --ignore-engines
solutions.explanation: Force install despite engine mismatch (use with caution).

solutions.title: Update package.json engines
solutions.explanation: Adjust the engines field in package.json if you control the package.

rules.regex: EPERM: operation not permitted|EACCES: permission denied

solutions.title: Fix npm cache permissions
solutions.explanation: Fix ownership of the npm cache directory.

solutions.title: Don't use sudo
solutions.explanation: Never use sudo with npm install. Use nvm or similar for local Node.

solutions.title: Clear npm cache
solutions.explanation: Corrupted cache can cause permission errors.

rules.regex: npm ERR! Invalid JSON|JSON.parse|Unexpected token

solutions.title: Validate package.json
solutions.explanation: Check for JSON syntax errors in package.json.

solutions.title: Check for trailing commas
solutions.explanation: JSON does not allow trailing commas. Remove them.

solutions.title: Use a JSON validator
solutions.explanation: Use jsonlint or an IDE to find and fix syntax errors.

rules.regex: self signed certificate|UNABLE_TO_VERIFY_LEAF_SIGNATURE|certificate has expired

solutions.title: Use strict-ssl false (dev only)
solutions.explanation: Only for development behind corporate proxy. Never in production.

solutions.title: Add CA certificate
solutions.explanation: Configure NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS with your corporate CA cert.

solutions.title: Check system date
solutions.explanation: Expired system date can cause certificate validation to fail.

rules.regex: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named '(.+)'

solutions.title: Install the missing package
solutions.explanation: The Python package is not installed. Add it to requirements.txt and install.

solutions.title: Update requirements.txt
solutions.explanation: Make sure the package is listed in your requirements.txt file.

solutions.title: Check virtual environment
solutions.explanation: Ensure your CI is using the correct virtual environment with all dependencies.

rules.regex: SyntaxError: (.+) \((.+), line (\d+)\)

solutions.title: Fix syntax error at specified line
solutions.explanation: There's a syntax error in your code. Check the specified file and line number.

solutions.title: Check for missing brackets or quotes
solutions.explanation: Common causes include missing parentheses, brackets, or unclosed strings.

solutions.title: Check Python version compatibility
solutions.explanation: Some syntax may not be supported in older Python versions.

rules.regex: pip._vendor.urllib3.exceptions.ReadTimeoutError|ReadTimeoutError

solutions.title: Increase pip timeout
solutions.explanation: The network connection is slow. Increase the default timeout for pip.

rules.regex: SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED|ssl.SSLCertVerificationError

solutions.title: Use trusted host (dev only)
solutions.explanation: Bypass SSL verification for dev/corporate proxy. Avoid in production.

solutions.title: Update certificates
solutions.explanation: Update the certifi package for latest CA bundle.

solutions.title: Configure NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS
solutions.explanation: If behind corporate proxy, add the CA cert to trusted store.

rules.regex: error: command 'gcc' failed|Microsoft Visual C\+\+ .* required|error: Microsoft Visual C\+\+ 14\.0 or greater is required

solutions.title: Install build essentials (Linux)
solutions.explanation: Install gcc, g++, make for compiling C extensions.

solutions.title: Install Visual C++ (Windows)
solutions.explanation: Install Microsoft C++ Build Tools from Visual Studio.

solutions.title: Use prebuilt wheels
solutions.explanation: Prefer packages with prebuilt wheels to avoid compilation.

rules.regex: Invalid requirement|Parse error|Expected version spec

solutions.title: Fix requirements syntax
solutions.explanation: Use valid pip format: package==1.0.0 or package>=1.0,<2.0

solutions.title: Check for typos
solutions.explanation: Package names are case-sensitive. Verify spelling.

solutions.title: Validate with pip
solutions.explanation: Validate requirements file without installing.

rules.regex: No module named 'venv'|The virtual environment was not created

solutions.title: Install venv
solutions.explanation: On Debian/Ubuntu, install the venv package.

solutions.title: Use virtualenv
solutions.explanation: Use virtualenv as an alternative to venv.

solutions.title: Use conda
solutions.explanation: Use conda create for environment management.

rules.regex: pip's dependency resolver does not currently take into account|Consider upgrading pip

solutions.title: Upgrade pip
solutions.explanation: Upgrade pip to the latest version.

solutions.title: Use python -m pip
solutions.explanation: Ensure you're upgrading the correct pip.

rules.regex: error: subprocess-exited-with-error|Building wheel for .* failed

solutions.title: Install build dependencies
solutions.explanation: Ensure build and wheel are installed.

solutions.title: Check compiler
solutions.explanation: C extensions need gcc/Visual C++. See python_compiler_missing pattern.

solutions.title: Use prebuilt wheel
solutions.explanation: Install from wheel if available: pip install package.whl

rules.regex: ImportError: (.+)|ImportError: cannot import name '(.+)'

solutions.title: Install missing dependency
solutions.explanation: The imported module or package is not installed.

solutions.title: Check circular imports
solutions.explanation: Circular imports can cause ImportError. Restructure the code.

solutions.title: Check PYTHONPATH
solutions.explanation: Ensure the module's directory is in PYTHONPATH.

rules.regex: fatal: Authentication failed for '(.+)'

solutions.title: Configure GitHub token
solutions.explanation: Set up a GitHub token in your CI secrets and configure git to use it.

solutions.title: Check repository permissions
solutions.explanation: Ensure the token has the required permissions to access the repository.

solutions.title: Use SSH authentication
solutions.explanation: Consider using SSH keys instead of HTTPS for authentication.

rules.regex: protected branch hook declined|cannot push to protected branch

solutions.title: Check branch protection rules
solutions.explanation: You are trying to push directly to a protected branch (e.g., main). Create a PR instead.

rules.regex: CONFLICT \(|Merge conflict in|Automatic merge failed

solutions.title: Resolve conflict markers
solutions.explanation: Edit files to remove <<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>> and keep the correct code.

solutions.title: Abort merge
solutions.explanation: Abort the merge and try a different approach.

solutions.title: Use merge tool
solutions.explanation: Use a merge tool to resolve conflicts visually.

rules.regex: submodule.*failed|fatal: .*submodule|Unable to find current

solutions.title: Init and update submodules
solutions.explanation: Initialize and fetch all submodules.

solutions.title: Check submodule URL
solutions.explanation: Ensure .gitmodules has correct URLs and you have access.

solutions.title: Use token for private submodules
solutions.explanation: Configure Git credentials for private submodule repos.

rules.regex: batch request failed|LFS not found|This repository is configured for Git LFS

solutions.title: Install Git LFS
solutions.explanation: Install and initialize Git LFS in the repository.

solutions.title: Pull LFS files
solutions.explanation: Explicitly pull LFS files after clone.

solutions.title: Check LFS in CI
solutions.explanation: Ensure Git LFS is installed and run before checkout in CI.

patterns.name: Git Shallow Clone Update Not Allowed

rules.regex: shallow update not allowed|cannot rebase: Your index contains uncommitted changes

solutions.title: Unshallow the repo
solutions.explanation: Convert shallow clone to full clone.

solutions.title: Don't use shallow in CI
solutions.explanation: Remove fetch-depth: 1 if it causes issues with merge/rebase.

solutions.title: Use full clone
solutions.explanation: In actions/checkout, set fetch-depth: 0 for full history.

rules.regex: Unable to create '.*/refs/heads/.*\.lock'|Could not create directory

solutions.title: Remove stale lock
solutions.explanation: Remove stale lock files from crashed git process.

solutions.title: Check concurrent processes
solutions.explanation: Ensure only one git process is writing at a time.

solutions.title: Fix permissions
solutions.explanation: Ensure .git directory is writable.

rules.regex: ! \[remote rejected\].*|remote: error: refusing to

solutions.title: Pull and rebase
solutions.command_template: git pull --rebase origin main && git push
solutions.explanation: Remote has new commits. Rebase and push.

solutions.title: Force push (use with caution)
solutions.explanation: Only if you're sure. Prefer --force-with-lease over --force.

solutions.title: Check branch protection
solutions.explanation: Protected branches may reject direct pushes. Create a PR.

rules.regex: AssertionError: (.+)

solutions.title: Review the failing test
solutions.explanation: The test assertion failed. Review expected vs actual values in the test output.

solutions.title: Check recent code changes
solutions.explanation: Recent changes might have broken expected behavior. Review the git diff.

solutions.title: Update test expectations
solutions.explanation: If the new behavior is correct, update the test expectations.

rules.regex: TimeoutError|Timeout expired|timed out after (\d+)

solutions.title: Increase test timeout
solutions.explanation: The test is taking too long. Consider increasing the timeout value.

solutions.title: Optimize slow test
solutions.explanation: The test might be doing too much. Consider mocking external dependencies.

solutions.title: Check for infinite loops
solutions.explanation: The code might have an infinite loop or deadlock. Review the test logic.

rules.regex: Coverage threshold|coverage for .* failed|global coverage

solutions.title: Add missing tests
solutions.explanation: Write tests to cover the uncovered code.

solutions.title: Adjust threshold temporarily
solutions.explanation: Lower the threshold in jest.config or similar while adding tests.

solutions.title: Exclude non-critical paths
solutions.explanation: Exclude certain files from coverage if they're not critical.

rules.regex: retries exhausted|flaky|test failed on retry

solutions.title: Fix test determinism
solutions.explanation: Remove timing dependencies, randomness, or external state from the test.

solutions.title: Add proper mocks
solutions.explanation: Mock external services and time-dependent code.

solutions.title: Increase retries as temporary fix
solutions.explanation: Use retries only as a last resort while fixing the root cause.

rules.regex: Error: Cannot find module|setup.*failed|beforeAll.*failed

solutions.title: Install test dependencies
solutions.explanation: Ensure test runner and dependencies are installed.

solutions.title: Check test configuration
solutions.explanation: Verify jest.config, pytest.ini, or equivalent is correct.

solutions.title: Set NODE_ENV
solutions.explanation: Some apps require NODE_ENV=test for proper setup.

rules.regex: MemoryError|JavaScript heap out of memory|ENOMEM

solutions.title: Increase memory limit
solutions.explanation: The process ran out of memory. Increase the available memory.

solutions.title: Optimize memory usage
solutions.explanation: Review code for memory leaks or inefficient memory usage.

solutions.title: Use streaming for large data
solutions.explanation: For large files or data, consider using streaming instead of loading everything into memory.

rules.regex: ImagePullBackOff|ErrImagePull|rpc error: code = NotFound

solutions.title: Check image name and tag
solutions.explanation: The image specified in the manifest does not exist or the tag is incorrect. Verify the image name and tag in your deployment.

solutions.title: Verify image pull secrets
solutions.explanation: If the image is private, ensure the ImagePullSecret is correctly configured in your namespace and referenced in the pod spec.

rules.regex: Error: Error acquiring the state lock.*ID: ([a-f0-9-]+)

solutions.title: Force unlock Terraform state
solutions.explanation: The state is locked by another process. If you are sure no other CLI is running, use force-unlock with the lock ID.

rules.regex: Could not resolve dependencies for project (.+): Could not find artifact (.+)

solutions.title: Update Maven local repository
solutions.explanation: Force an update of snapshots and releases from remote repositories.

rules.regex: error: the lock file (.+) needs to be updated but --frozen was passed

solutions.title: Update Cargo.lock
solutions.explanation: The lockfile is out of sync with Cargo.toml. Run cargo update or remove the frozen flag in CI.

rules.regex: verifying (.+): checksum mismatch

solutions.title: Clean Go mod cache and tidy
solutions.explanation: Corruption detected in the go.sum file or local cache. Tidy the modules to recalculate hashes.
